seoTitle: How to Record Personal Expenses & Owner Draws in QBO
metaDescription: Learn how to record personal expenses and owner draws in QuickBooks Online so business and personal transactions stay clearly separated.
intro: Mixing personal spending with business transactions is a common headache for small business owners, and QuickBooks Online has a clean way to handle it. This guide covers how to record personal expenses and owner draws correctly.

steps.heading: Set up an owner's draw account
steps.body: Create an equity account specifically for tracking owner draws if one doesn't already exist.

steps.heading: Identify the personal transaction
steps.body: Find the transaction in your bank feed or records that represents a personal expense paid from business funds.

steps.heading: Categorize it as an owner's draw
steps.body: Assign the transaction to the owner's draw account instead of a business expense category.

steps.heading: Review your equity reports
steps.body: Check your balance sheet or equity report to confirm the draw is reflected correctly.

faq.question: Is an owner's draw the same as a paycheck?
faq.answer: No, a draw is a withdrawal of equity rather than payroll, and the two are treated differently for tax purposes.

faq.question: Should personal expenses be categorized as business expenses?
faq.answer: No, personal expenses paid from a business account should be recorded as an owner's draw rather than a business expense.

faq.question: Does an owner's draw affect business profit?
faq.answer: No, draws reduce owner's equity but don't appear on the profit and loss statement as an expense.

faq.question: Can multiple owners each have their own draw account?
faq.answer: Yes, in a multi-owner business, it's common to set up separate draw accounts for each partner or member.
